era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Egypt's Old Kingdom (Fifth Dynasty) is consolidating power after the Fourth Dynasty. Mesopotamian city-states continue competing for dominance. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature, urban phase. Writing and administrative systems are well-established across the Near East.

events.title: Transition within Fourth Dynasty Egypt (uncertain event)
events.description: This year falls within the contested chronology of the Fourth Dynasty. Depending on which chronological system is adopted, this year may fall during the reign of Khafre, Menkaure, or the transition between them. No specific event is attested for this year in surviving sources. The year is included to acknowledge the period's historical significance, even in the absence of documented events.
events.certainty_note: No specific event is documented for -2831. The assignment of this year to any particular pharaoh is speculative and depends entirely on which chronological system is adopted.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for -2831 in any source tradition. The year is historically 'empty' in the surviving record. This underscores the sparseness of documentation for the Old Kingdom and the dependence on king lists whose absolute dating is uncertain.
historiographic_note: For -2831, no contemporary sources document specific events. The historical record is essentially silent. This is typical for most years in the Old Kingdom; events are known only through later king lists and archaeological stratigraphy. The absence of documentation does not indicate the absence of historical activity, only the absence of surviving records.
